skill.name: api-documentation
skill.description: API documentation workflow for generating OpenAPI specs, creating developer guides, and maintaining comprehensive API documentation.
skill.summary: Guides teams through API discovery, OpenAPI specification, developer guides, examples, interactive docs, and maintenance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks as Ruby shell execution and text substrings as weak cryptographic algorithms. Manual review found only documentation workflow instructions, companion skill names, and copy-paste prompts; no executable code, network access, filesystem access, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Treated as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are inline markdown skill names and fenced copy-paste prompts. They do not define Ruby code, shell commands, scripts, or runtime execution paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is markdown documentation, and every flagged backtick marks a skill name or prompt block. There is no interpreter directive or executable code context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The high-severity cryptography alerts match ordinary documentation words such as description, design, and API guidance. The skill does not implement hashing, encryption, or authentication code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic algorithm usage at the flagged lines. The alerts are substring matches inside normal documentation text.

content.value_statement: API teams often miss endpoints, examples, or maintenance steps when documentation is created manually. This skill provides a phased workflow for OpenAPI specs, developer guides, interactive docs, and upkeep.
actual_capabilities: Organizes API documentation work into discovery, specification, guide writing, examples, interactive docs, site planning, and maintenance phases.
Prompts use companion skills for endpoint discovery, OpenAPI specification creation, developer guides, examples, and documentation architecture.
Lists concrete documentation tasks, including request and response documentation, authentication notes, error codes, and rate limits.
Includes quality gates for OpenAPI completeness, developer guides, working examples, interactive docs, and deployment readiness.
Supports planning for Swagger UI, Redoc, API portals, search, navigation, validation, and review processes.
limitations: It is a workflow guide, not an automated OpenAPI parser or documentation generator.
It depends on companion skills named in the workflow being available in the user's environment.
It does not verify API behavior, execute tests, or deploy documentation by itself.
It provides generic phases and prompts, so project-specific standards must be supplied by the user.

use_cases.title: Document a New Public API
use_cases.description: Create a complete documentation plan before release, including endpoint inventory, authentication guidance, examples, and interactive reference pages.

use_cases.title: Convert Internal Notes to OpenAPI
use_cases.description: Turn scattered endpoint notes into a structured OpenAPI specification and developer guide with clear quality checks.

use_cases.title: Improve an Existing Developer Portal
use_cases.description: Review missing examples, navigation, search, interactive docs, and maintenance workflows for an established API site.

prompt_templates.title: Start an API Documentation Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this API documentation workflow to create a phased plan for documenting my API. Include endpoint discovery, OpenAPI work, guides, examples, and quality gates.

prompt_templates.title: Create an OpenAPI Documentation Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply this workflow to build a checklist for producing an OpenAPI specification. Include paths, schemas, authentication, error handling, examples, and review steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Developer Guide
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to outline a developer guide for my API. Cover getting started, authentication, common patterns, troubleshooting, examples, and FAQ content.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Full API Documentation Program
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to audit our API documentation program. Assess discovery, OpenAPI coverage, guides, examples, interactive docs, portal structure, automation, validation, and maintenance.

output_examples.input: Plan documentation for a payments API before public launch.
output: Endpoint inventory with authentication, request fields, response fields, errors, and rate limits.
OpenAPI specification plan with schemas, paths, examples, and security definitions.
Developer guide outline with onboarding steps, common payment flows, troubleshooting, and FAQ topics.
Quality gates for complete specs, tested examples, interactive docs, and deployment readiness.

output_examples.input: Improve documentation for an existing internal API.
output: Gap analysis across endpoint coverage, examples, error documentation, navigation, and search.
Recommended updates for interactive documentation and developer portal structure.
Maintenance process covering validation, review cadence, feedback monitoring, and automated generation.

best_practices: Provide current endpoint lists, authentication rules, error models, and rate limits before using the workflow.
Validate generated examples against real API behavior before publishing them.
Keep OpenAPI specs, developer guides, and interactive docs updated through the same review process.
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated documentation without checking endpoint accuracy and example behavior.
Do not treat this workflow as a substitute for API testing or security review.
Do not skip maintenance planning after the first documentation release.

faq.question: Does this skill generate an OpenAPI file automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a workflow and prompts for producing an OpenAPI specification with supporting tools.

faq.question: Can it document private or internal APIs?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the API structure and internal standards, then use the workflow to organize the documentation work.

faq.question: Does it include developer guide planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes phases for getting started guides, authentication, common patterns, troubleshooting, and FAQ content.

faq.question: Does it support interactive documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow includes planning for Swagger UI, Redoc, try-it functionality, and deployment checks.

faq.question: What tools does it require?
faq.answer: It is written for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Some phases reference companion skills that must be available separately.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual audit found no executable code, network calls, filesystem operations, or prompt injection attempts.
